scoring.note: provisionalScore uses a deterministic heuristic for the intelligence half. The agent should replace heuristicIntelligenceScore with its own 6-dimension LLM evaluation, then compute finalScore = round(0.5*foundationalScore + 0.5*llmIntelligenceScore).

checks.label: AI bot access
checks.details: No AI bots blocked in robots.txt.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add structured data (JSON-LD) on key templates
prioritizedFixes.why: Agents rely on machine-readable entities and page intent signals.

prioritizedFixes.title: Standardize title and meta description quality
prioritizedFixes.why: Weak metadata reduces retrieval accuracy and snippet quality.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add an RSS or Atom feed
prioritizedFixes.why: RSS feeds help AI systems discover and track new content.

prioritizedFixes.title: Improve content structure with clear heading levels
prioritizedFixes.why: Well-structured H1 → H2 → H3 outlines help AI agents chunk, extract, and cite specific sections.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add recognized schema types to JSON-LD
prioritizedFixes.why: Named types like Organization, Product, Article help AI classify your content.

prioritizedFixes.title: Create a docs/knowledge section for product facts
prioritizedFixes.why: A stable docs corpus helps agents extract precise answers.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Does the content lead with direct answers to questions agents get asked?
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 0% pages have Q&A-style headings; 25% lead with definitions.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Date signals, update timestamps, and active maintenance indicators.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 38% pages have date signals; no RSS feed.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Can an agent extract clean, self-contained passages to quote as citations?
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 75% pages have rich headings; 100% meet depth threshold.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Clean semantic HTML structure that survives extraction to markdown.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 63% clean heading hierarchy; 100% have single H1.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Density of statistics, data points, named sources, and in-text citations.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 88% pages include numeric evidence; 0% have author attribution.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Substantive long-form content that covers topics with depth and breadth.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 100% pages meet depth threshold across 4 template types.

aiView.title: We're hiring – Linear
aiView.metaDescription: We are crafting the tool for teams who care about quality. Join us on our mission!
aiView.textExcerpt: Help us craft high-quality tools We are building a new species of product tool. Will you help us make it better? Open roles ↓ Elapsed 00:00 Seek to: 00:00 / Duration 00:00 Remaining − 00:00 0.25 × 0.5 × 0.75 × 1 × 1.25 × 1.5 × 1.75 × 2 × On a quest for quality We believe that there is a lost art of building software. A craftsmanship that yields products of an exceptional, almost magical, quality. It’s difficult to describe this quality with words and impossible to measure it in numbers, but you can feel it when it’s there. You know it when you experience it. To bring back this level of quality, we are building a tool that empowers product teams to do their best work. A tool with unparalleled speed, focus, and design. Building this tool requires a team of talented individuals who care deeply about the quality of their work, no matter if it’s engineering, design, or customer support. We are looking for people who share our passion for software craftsmanship and getting even the smallest details right. If that sounds like you, please reach out and join us on our quest. ›_ README A conversation on quality Karri Saarinen → Building the sync engine Tuomas Artman → How we build products Lenny’s Podcast → At home with Karri Saarinen Indie → Move fast with little process The Pragmatic Engineer → A conversation on quality Karri Saarinen Building the sync engine Tuomas Artman How we build products Lenny’s Podcast At home with Karri Saarinen Indie Move fast with little process The Pragmatic Engineer How we think and work Linear’s mission is to inspire and accelerate builders. To turn that ambition into reality, we operate based on a set of core principles that keep us focused. 01 . Ship early Simplify and ship smaller. 02 . Build with users Listen carefully and understand their problems. 03 . Know what good looks like Find and create good things. Know what needs to change to make it better. 04 . Think in principles Not in playbooks. 05 . Build things that last Put care into the

aiView.textExcerpt: Now All Changelog Community News Craft AI Practices Press Search… Teaching an agent to auto-fix bugs Some bugs at Linear now get fixed before a single engineer picks them up. With Coding Sessions, it can take a bug from triage all the way to a reviewed fix, without the work ever leaving Linear. Karri Saarinen · Jun 11, 2026 → Reviewing code in the agent era Agentic coding has puts real pressure on review. One of our engineers shares how he uses Linear Diffs to hold his bar high without drowning in pull requests. Maciek Pekala · Jun 10, 2026 → Code review should be fast Agents are forcing a change in how we review code. Diffs brings code reviews directly inside Linear, so that the review lives alongside all of your product context. Tuomas Artman · May 28, 2026 → Code Intelligence for Linear Agent Linear Agent can now read your codebase and answer questions from the source itself. Karri Saarinen · May 14, 2026 → Cars24’s push to being AI-native with Linear In October, Cars24 renewed its Jira license. A few weeks later, they treated that contract as a sunk cost and walked away from it. Customer story · May 13, 2026 → Changelog Initiative properties Initiatives define your company's high-level goals and organize the projects that contribute to them. To help you manage initiatives as your roadmap grows, we've added a new set of focused initiative properties: Jun 30, 2026 Agent assisted project updates Project and initiative updates keep teams aligned, but writing them means pulling out recent changes from issues, documents, and discussions. Jun 17, 2026 Coding sessions in Linear Earlier this year, we launched Linear Agent, giving teams a new way to plan and coordinate their issues and projects. Since then, we've added Code Intelligence to extend that understanding to the codebase, and MCP su

headings: Centrally manage access to Linear’s MCP server in Claude ⁠
Desktop navigation history and pinned tabs ⁠
A coding agent your team will love ⁠
aiView.textExcerpt: Now All Changelog Community News Craft AI Practices Press Search… July 2, 2026 Initiative properties Initiatives define your company’s high-level goals and organize the projects that contribute to them. To help you manage initiatives as your roadmap grows, we’ve added a new set of focused initiative properties: Proposed and canceled statuses make it clear when an initiative is under consideration or has been intentionally set aside Priority communicates which initiatives are most important Labels group initiatives by product line, region, or other meaningful categories Use these properties to group and filter initiatives on any plan, or build custom initiative views on Enterprise plans. Learn more about initiative properties in the docs . Centrally manage access to Linear’s MCP server in Claude ⁠ Give employees secure access to Linear and other MCP servers in Claude, without requiring them to authenticate with each server individually. Enterprise-managed Authorization lets Okta administrators manage access and permissions centrally, and users automatically get access to the MCP servers they’re authorized to use when they sign in to Claude. Enterprise-Managed Authorization is available to Enterprise workspaces using Okta as an identity provider and Claude as an MCP client. Learn more in the docs or in the MCP specification . Share filtered views ⁠ You can now share any filtered view by copying its URL from your browser, or by pressing ⌘ / Ctrl Shift C on desktop. Share filtered views to discuss a subset of an existing view with someone else — like an issue view filtered by an assignee, or project or initiative view filtered by a label. Fixes Agents Fixed issues with editing collapsible sections inside documents and descriptions with the Linear agent API Stopped automatically cancelling and restarting active Triage Intelligence runs after issue content changes API Fixed fieldless GraphQL input metadata from turning persisted filters into match-all filters Asks Slack /

aiView.title: About – Linear
aiView.metaDescription: We’re bringing magic back to software.
h1: Building tools for the next era of product development
headings: A new species of product tool
Built for the future. Available today.
aiView.textExcerpt: Building tools for the next era of product development AI is fundamentally changing how products get built. We are shaping what comes next. Elapsed 00:00 Seek to: 00:00 / Duration 00:00 Remaining − 00:00 0.25 × 0.5 × 0.75 × 1 × 1.25 × 1.5 × 1.75 × 2 × A new species of product tool Software development is at an inflection point. Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping how products are built. AI increases what teams can create, but it also raises the bar for clarity and coordination. At Linear, we are building the tools for this new era of product development. A purpose-built system where teams and agents operate together in a shared, structured environment. Founded in 2019, Linear has become the tool of choice for more than 33,000 companies (including OpenAI, Coinbase, and Ramp) to plan, build, and ship their products. Our team is distributed across North America and Europe, and we’re continuing to grow internationally. What unites us is relentless focus, fast execution, and a deep care for software craftsmanship. Meet the team behind Linear We are designers and engineers. Problem solvers and storytellers. We are a diverse team of individuals, all makers at heart. We’re hiring → Karri Saarinen Co-founder, CEO Jori Lallo Co-founder, CPO Tuomas Artman Co-founder, CTO Cristina Cordova COO Nan Yu Head of Product Tom Moor Head of Engineering Casey Bertenthal Head of Sales Jamie Finnigan Head of Security Tim Qi Matthew Roberts Matthijs Wolting Josh Pyles Nathalie Alex Dominic Wong Meg Wayne Mingjie Jiang Alan Doyle Alex Suevalov Anthony Vidalez Saneel Prabhu Igor Sechyn Axel Niklasson Yun Davina Baker Jon Phey Duong Vu Rhea Purohit Mufeez Amjad Tony Wooster Ben Kinney Simone Jacobs Zoe Wellner Bojan Joveski Yann-Edern Gillet Paul Macgregor Emiel Janson Dylan Hamilton Eitan Meisels Steven DeMartini Amelia Cellar Paco Coursey Kristin Boyer Peter Travers Maciek Pekala Skyline Lau Jesse Hartheimer Aaron Quinn Lena Vu Robb Böhnke Jacob Shumway Emil Kowalski Hilary Ho

aiView.title: Linear for Agents
aiView.metaDescription: Build and deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates.
headings: Fast, focused, and reliable. Just like the rest of your team.
Stay in the loop every step of the way
Agents for every use case, ready to deploy
aiView.textExcerpt: Linear for Agents Artificial colleagues. Natural collaboration. Build and deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates. Work on complex tasks together or delegate issues end-to-end. Watch demo Cursor Agent Cursor Agent GitHub Copilot Agent Sentry Agent Leela Codex Agent Conor Delegate, automate, and orchestrate work Choose from a variety of AI agents and start ­delegating work, from code generation to other ­technical tasks. Fast, focused, and reliable. Just like the rest of your team. Agents are full members of your Linear workspace. Assign them to issues, add them to projects, or @mention them in comments. Delegate issues, but not accountability When an issue gets delegated to an agent, the human user remains the primary assignee, while the agent is added as a contributor. Stay in the loop every step of the way Agents act on your behalf, but never in the dark. Understand every change they make at a glance, or inspect the underlying reasoning. Orchestrate tasks at scale Agents can work across multiple issues simultaneously. Select tasks, assign them to an agent, and watch work move forward.

aiView.textExcerpt: Coding Sessions Assign your issues to Linear Linear Agent can now write code using Claude Code and Codex. Triage, plan, build, and review all in one place. Changes: useRideHistory.ts : build a waitingStatusById map and use it as the getLastAction byline RideHistoryPage.tsx : dimmed rows reset to full opacity Tell Linear what to do next...
